user_query:
You're our contract event producer for a one-day hybrid B2B SaaS mini-summit in Austin in October 2026 targeting ~300 attendees. Read the inputs under input/ and produce the following outputs under output/:

1) output/event_brief.yaml — Complete the event brief with: type (conference/summit), format (hybrid), audience details, expected_size aligned to the inputs, SMART success metrics (tie registration targets to input), budget section reflecting the $60,000 cap, stakeholders, constraints, and at least three explicit kill_criteria (e.g., registration velocity, speaker confirmations, sponsor revenue) consistent with best practices.

2) output/venue_selection.yaml — Choose one venue from input/venues.csv. Include:
- chosen_venue: name (must exist in venues.csv)
- capacity_ok, wifi_required_mbps (compute attendees × 2), wifi_ok, ada_ok booleans
- checklist summary aligning to must-haves (capacity +10% buffer, WiFi, accessibility)
- justification: 3-5 bullet reasons for selection
- red_flags: at least 3 bullets summarizing why other venues were not chosen

3) output/budget.yaml — Use the provided budget template structure. Compute revenue from input/marketing_targets.json (ticket tiers and quantities) and input/sponsors.csv (tier prices × quantities). For expenses, set venue.rental from the chosen venue’s rental, and venue.catering as catering_per_person × expected attendees. Include reasonable placeholders for other expense lines but keep total_expenses within the $60,000 cap. Compute summary.net_result, summary.roi_percentage, cost_per_attendee, and break_even_registrations. Ensure all totals are correctly summed.

4) output/run_sheet.yaml — Use the master run sheet structure. Include the following team roles at minimum: Event Director, Registration Lead, AV/Tech Lead, Speaker Liaison, Catering Coordinator, Social Media / Content. Provide a day-of timeline with clear times that includes: team arrival, AV setup, registration desk setup, opening welcome, morning sessions, breaks at least every 90 minutes, lunch of at least 75 minutes, afternoon sessions, closing, and teardown. Include emergency_contacts with venue_manager and other relevant contacts.

5) output/marketing_emails.jsonl — Four JSONL entries representing the sequence: save_the_date, early_bird, speaker_spotlight, last_chance. Each line must include: {"type": "...", "subject": "...", "body": "...", "send_window": "T-90"|"T-75"|"T-60"|"T-7"}. Subjects should reflect each email’s purpose (e.g., “Save the date”, “Early bird”, “is joining…”, “Last chance” / “spots left”). Bodies should be concise and value-driven.

Use the inputs as the source of truth for targets, pricing, and venue facts. Keep decisions within constraints in input/constraints.yaml. Make sure calculations are explicit and consistent across files. All file paths must be exactly as specified above.
